original_abstract: Universal human values serve as the foundational bedrock for shaping national character and fostering a society characterized by justice, peace, and prosperity. In an era increasingly defined by the volatile forces of globalization and modernization, the early cultivation of these values has become more critical than ever. Within the Indonesian school system, Pancasila Education occupies a strategic role in this moral development. This study explores the profound compatibility between Hindu philosophy—rich with a diverse array of ethical tenets—and the core principles of Pancasila Education. By examining the potential for integrating Hindu philosophical concepts into the curriculum, this research highlights how such synergy can help the younger generation develop robust moral integrity and a deeper respect for collective humanity. While acknowledging the practical challenges and institutional obstacles to such integration, this paper argues that bridging these two domains offers immense long-term benefits for the spiritual and civic growth of Indonesia’s youth and the future stability of the nation.
humanized_abstract: Universal human values serve as the basic foundation to determine the character of the nation and also to build a just, peaceful, and prosperous society. Nowadays, in the midst of the turbulent development of globalization and modernization, values must be nurtured as early as possible. And in the Indonesian national education system, Pancasila Education is very influential to shape the students’ characters. Through empirical studies, this paper reveals in a very thorough manner the affinity of Hindu philosophy with plenty of values in accordance with the values of Pancasila Education, so that the values of Hindu Philosophy can be employed as a material to be incorporated in the curriculum of Pancasila Education, in order to endow students with high moral values as well as global humanity values that are expected to be stronger and able to develop the nation in the future. This paper discusses the correspondence between Hindu philosophy and values of Pancasila Education, as well as revealing the opportunities and challenges that arise in incorporating values in Hindu philosophy as material in the curriculum of Pancasila Education. The result of this research is hoped to reveal many opportunities for the future development of character education of Indonesian students and is expected to contribute for the prosperity of the nation in the future.
